answer:
In Allama Iqbal's *Shikwa* (from *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*), the speaker—representing the Muslim ummah—directly addresses God (referred to as "O God" or implied through context) with a lament about their past glories contrasted against their current degradation, disgrace, and loss of worldly favor to non-Muslims/infidel foes.[^1]

One key opening verse establishing the direct complaint:

> Forgive my tongue, but God, I have a grievance to declare.[^2]

This builds into explicit grievances about Muslims' fate, such as:

> But all Your blessings fall upon the houses of the foe,  
> While on the wretched Muslims falls the lightning’s fiery blow.[^3]

> The idols in the temples say, “The Muslims now are gone!”  
> They celebrate that guardians of the Kaaba have withdrawn.  
> ...  
> The unbelievers mock us now—do You feel this or not?  
> The Creed of Your own Oneness—have You utterly forgot?[^4]

> The outrage is that infidels get nymphs and mansions there,  
> While for the wretched Muslim, just the promise of a prayer.  
> ...  
> Why is the wealth of this world now so scarce for Muslim hands?  
> ...  
> We face the taunts of strangers, disgrace, and poverty;  
> Is this the prize for those who gave their very lives for Thee?[^5]

> The world has now become the friend of those who are not ours,  
> And for ourselves, a world of dreams is all that now empowers.  
> We have departed from the stage; others possess its towers.[^6]

The full poem elaborates this theme throughout, juxtaposing Muslim heroism (e.g., conquering for God's name) with their present subjugation.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 20 "Shikwa" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/)).

[^2]: Ibid., [p-7932bc](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-7932bc).

[^3]: Ibid., [p-20fc7c](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-20fc7c).

[^4]: Ibid., [p-c09121](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-c09121).

[^5]: Ibid., [p-45880c](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-45880c) to [p-d1e7fe](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-d1e7fe).

[^6]: Ibid., [p-bd43b7](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-bd43b7).
